title: BLE disconnect callback not registered for custom client factories
description:
File: python/src/lichen/client/ble.py, lines 249-262

In BlePacketTransport._build_client(), when a custom client_factory is provided, the disconnected_callback is never registered:

```python
def _build_client(self) -> BleClientLike:
    if self._client_factory is not None:
        return self._client_factory(self.address)  # No disconnect callback!
    ...
    return cast(
        BleClientLike,
        client_cls(self.address, disconnected_callback=self._on_disconnect),
    )
```

Impact: If the BLE device disconnects unexpectedly when using a custom client:
1. _connected stays True incorrectly
2. _on_disconnect is never called
3. packets() iterator hangs forever waiting for data that will never arrive
4. No sentinel (None) is put in the queue to terminate iteration

Fix: Provide a way for custom clients to register the disconnect callback, or document that custom clients MUST call a public disconnect notification method.
close_reason: Fixed: BleClientFactory now passes disconnect callback to custom clients
